content.problemStatement:
You are given **N planets** (represented as nodes) and **M connections** between some pairs of planets (represented as undirected edges).

A **group** (connected component) is defined as a set of planets where each planet is reachable from any other planet in the same set via the given connections. Planets in different groups cannot reach each other through the existing connections.

Your task is to **find the number of disconnected groups (connected components) in the galaxy network** based on the given connections.


Input Format

The first line contains two space-separated integers, `N` and `M`, representing the number of planets and connections.
Planets are numbered from `1` to `N`.
The next `M` lines describe the connections, each containing two integers, `u` and `v`, representing a bidirectional connection between planets `u` and `v`.
Each connection links two distinct planets, and at most one connection exists between any two planets.


Output Format

On a single line, print a single integer - the number of connected components.
constraints: At most one connection between any pair of planets
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with a line containing two integers N and M – the number of planets and the number of connections. The next M lines each contain two integers u and v (1‑based), denoting an undirected connection between planets u and v.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the number of connected components in the given galaxy network.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: all five planets are connected, so one component. Test case 2: edges (1,2) and (3,4) create two groups, no isolated planets, so two components. Test case 3: a chain connects all six planets, giving one component.
No connections exist, each of the four planets forms its own component.
Component A = {1,2,3}, Component B = {4,5,6}, Component C = {7} (isolated).

hidden.label: tricky case (multiple components with isolated nodes)

solution.approach: The planets and connections form an undirected graph. Two planets belong to the same group iff they are in the same connected component. We can maintain a Disjoint Set Union (DSU) structure: initially each planet is its own set. For every connection (u,v) we unite the sets containing u and v. After processing all edges, the number of distinct roots in the DSU equals the number of connected components. DSU with union by size (or rank) and path compression works in almost O(1) amortised time, giving overall O(N+M) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N + M) per test case (amortised), O(total N + total M) for the whole input
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for the DSU arrays per test case
